fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Delaware Dietitian License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Delaware - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: dietitian license reciprocity delaware, how to transfer dietitian license to delaware, dietitian compact delaware, delaware dietitian endorsement application

board.name: Board of Dietetics/Nutrition
board.mailing_address:
Cannon Building, 861 Silver Lake Blvd, Suite 203
Dover, DE 19904
board.physical_address:
Cannon Building, 861 Silver Lake Blvd, Suite 203
Dover, DE 19904

quick_facts.renewal_cycle: Every 2 years (expires May 31 of odd years) + 30 CE hours

license_types.title: Licensed Dietitian/Nutritionist (LDN)
license_types.description: The credential to practice dietetics or nutrition in Delaware, regulated by the Board of Dietetics/Nutrition under the Division of Professional Regulation.

steps.title: Complete Education & Supervised Practice
steps.description: Earn a master's degree or higher from an ACEND-accredited program. Complete a minimum of 1,200 hours of board-approved supervised practice through a dietetic internship.

steps.title: Pass the CDR Examination
steps.description: Successfully pass the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) Registration Examination for Dietitians (or the CNS exam for Nutritionists). Verification of current RD/CNS status is required.

steps.title: Submit the Application via DELPROS
steps.description: Create an account in the Delaware Professional Regulation Online Services (DELPROS) system, complete the application, and pay the $235 fee.

steps.title: Provide Verification Documents
steps.description: Upload or have primary source verification sent for your active CDR card or CNS certification. Any existing out-of-state licenses must be verified as well.

documents.detail: Completed application submitted electronically with the $235 fee.
Proof of active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration.
Verification of your accredited degree, usually requested directly through DELPROS processing.

provisional_text: Delaware may issue a temporary license to an applicant who has completed the educational and experience requirements and is awaiting the results of the next scheduled examination. Practice must be supervised.
reciprocity_text: Delaware offers licensure by reciprocity. Out-of-state applicants with an active license in good standing from a jurisdiction with substantially equivalent standards (or those holding active CDR/CNS credentials) can apply via the DELPROS reciprocity application.

faqs.question: How much does a Delaware dietitian license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial cost is a $235 application processing fee.

faqs.question: When does the Delaware license need to be renewed?
faqs.answer: Delaware dietitian licenses expire biennially on May 31 of odd-numbered years. Renewal fees are determined dynamically based on administrative costs.

faqs.question: Does Delaware license Nutritionists distinct from Dietitians?
faqs.answer: Delaware uses the combined title of Licensed Dietitian/Nutritionist. Applicants can qualify via either the Registered Dietitian (CDR) pathway or the Certified Nutrition Specialist (CNS) pathway.

faqs.question: Does Delaware have reciprocity for dietitians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Delaware. To work in Delaware, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Delaware?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 3 to 5 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Delaware while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Delaware offers licensure by reciprocity. Out-of-state applicants with an active license in good standing from a jurisdiction with substantially equivalent standards (or those holding active CDR/CNS credentials) can apply via the DELPROS reciprocity application.
